question.text: How often must a Regulated Entity update the CDD information for different risk categories of customers?

answer.text: At least once every two years for high-risk customers, once every eight years for medium-risk customers, and once every ten years for low-risk customers
Every year for all customers regardless of risk category
Only when the customer specifically requests a review of their records
Only at the time of account opening and never again during the relationship

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under IFSCA KYC norms, the periodicity of CDD updates is risk-based: high-risk customers require updates at least once every two years, medium-risk at least once every eight years, and low-risk at least once every ten years. Where the risk categorization by a Financial Group entity differs from the Regulated Entity's, the stricter periodicity applies. **The Trap:** Option A (every year for all) is too frequent and not risk-differentiated. The risk-based approach means higher-risk customers get more frequent reviews. **Cross-Exam Context:** CDD update periodicity is a specific testable detail in NISM IFSCA-01. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Document your CDD update policy clearly and set automated reminders — missing periodic KYC reviews is a common compliance finding during audits.
